title: Module 6 MongoDB
subtitle: Test your knowledge on MongoDB fundamentals, installation, commands, Express.js, and EJS templating.

questions.t: This popular NoSQL database is known for its flexibility, scalability, and performance, and is an open-source document-oriented database.
MongoDB uses this binary format (Binary JSON) to query the database and store data as documents.
This MongoDB architecture feature allows you to meet increasing demand by adding more nodes to share the load.
The user running MongoDB services (mongod, mongos) must belong to 'Performance Monitor Users' and this other group.
This MongoDB feature provides efficient search and data processing in very little time.
This MongoDB feature scales the database horizontally by partitioning data across multiple servers.
This MongoDB feature ensures high availability by maintaining multiple copies of data across servers, protecting the database if an active server goes down.
This MongoDB feature processes data records and returns a computed result, similar to SQL's GROUP BY clause.
In MongoDB, this is a container for collections, and each one gets its own set of files on the file system.
In MongoDB, this is a group of documents, similar to a table in relational databases.
In MongoDB, this is a data record stored as a BSON object composed of field-value pairs, analogous to a row in a relational database.
In a MongoDB document, this is a key-value pair that is analogous to a column in a relational database.
This MongoDB command creates a new database if it doesn't exist, or switches to it if it does. Syntax: _____ database_name
This MongoDB command lists all databases and their size on disk.
This MongoDB method is used to create a new collection, accepting a name and an optional options document for configuration (e.g., capped, autoIndexID, size).
This MongoDB command lists all collections in the current database.
This MongoDB method permanently deletes the current database, including all its collections and data.
This MongoDB method permanently deletes a single collection and all of its documents.
This MongoDB method inserts a single document into a collection.
This MongoDB method inserts multiple documents into a collection at once.
This MongoDB method deletes a single document matching the specified filter from a collection.
This MongoDB method deletes all documents matching the specified filter from a collection.
This MongoDB method retrieves documents from a collection. When called without arguments, it returns all documents.
This method is chained to find() to retrieve all documents in a more readable, formatted output.

This minimal and flexible Node.js web application framework provides a robust set of features for building web and mobile applications, and is often called the de facto standard server framework for Node.js.
In Express.js, these functions can execute code, modify request/response objects, end the request-response cycle, or call the next function in the stack.
Express.js provides a robust mechanism to define these using app.get(), app.post(), app.put(), and app.delete() for different HTTP methods.
Express.js supports Pug, EJS, and Handlebars among others for this purpose — generating dynamic HTML on the server side.
This Express.js middleware serves static files such as images, CSS, and JavaScript files.
This templating engine lets you generate HTML markup with plain JavaScript and is commonly used with Node.js and Express.js to build dynamic web pages.
In Express.js, this method is called to configure the template engine, as in: app.set('view engine', 'ejs').
In Express.js, this method is used to render a view template and send the rendered HTML string to the client, as in: res.render('index', { name: 'John Doe' }).
This EJS feature supports breaking templates into reusable components, reducing code duplication across views.
After setting environment variables for MongoDB on Windows, you need to copy this folder's path from the MongoDB installation location and add it to the system PATH.
These are the two MongoDB services that require the user to belong to Performance Monitor Users and Performance Log Users groups.
During MongoDB MSI installation, selecting this option installs all program features, as opposed to 'Custom' which lets you choose specific features.

MongoDB supports 14 data types. These include String, Integer, Boolean, Double, Min/Max Keys, Arrays, Timestamp, Null, Symbol, Date, Binary data, Code, Regular expression, and this type that serves as a unique identifier for each document.
This MongoDB data type stores an array of values (e.g., a list of groups a user belongs to) within a single document field.
This MongoDB data type is used to store a date or time value within a document.
This MongoDB data type records when a document was created or modified, useful for tracking changes over time.
These two MongoDB data types are used to compare a value against the lowest and highest BSON elements, respectively.
This MongoDB data type allows storing JavaScript code directly within a document.
This MongoDB data type allows storing pattern-matching expressions within a document, useful for search and validation.
MongoDB 4.4 or higher requires this architecture type — it does not support 32-bit systems.
In the MongoDB Community Server download discussed in class, this specific version number was selected for installation.
MongoDB supports many programming languages including Node.js, C, C++, C#, Java, Perl, PHP, Ruby, and this popular language known for data science and machine learning.
This Express.js feature allows you to define error-handling middleware to catch and respond to errors gracefully.
Express.js is described as highly _____ because it can be extended through custom or third-party middleware.
Express.js achieves high performance because it is built on Node.js's asynchronous, _____ architecture, allowing it to handle multiple simultaneous connections.
This EJS feature means it combines HTML and JavaScript in a way that is easy to learn and use.
This EJS feature allows it to generate HTML and JavaScript content dynamically within HTML tags.
